close_reason: Added task ble-setup with guided interactive flow
description: Setting up BLE requires knowing the sequence: submodule init -> install toolchain -> build firmware -> flash -> pair -> configure inject.method. The BLE quickstart in README documents it, but there's no 'task ble-setup' or similar that automates or guides this. Consider adding a task that chains fw-setup -> fw-build -> fw-flash -> ble-pair.
title: No single task for full BLE first-run setup

description: The packetNum field exists in the DataPacket wire protocol but the ESP32 never validates it — duplicate or out-of-order packets are accepted. Add monotonic counter tracking on the ESP32 side to reject replayed BLE packets. An attacker who captures encrypted packets could currently replay them to re-type the same text.
title: Add replay protection via monotonic counter validation on ESP32

close_reason: All changes applied to README.md
description: ESP-IDF requires Python 3.8+ but it is not listed in the firmware/esp32/README.md prerequisites section. The troubleshooting section references a Python path error but never lists Python as a prerequisite. Add it to the prerequisites list.
title: Add Python 3.8+ to firmware prerequisites

description: Create internal/hotkey package wrapping robotn/gohook for global keyboard event listening. Supports two modes: hold-to-talk (key down=start, key up=stop) and toggle (first press=start, second press=stop). Returns event channel and cancel function. Configurable key combination. Requires macOS Accessibility permission.
title: Global hotkey listener (gohook)

close_reason: All three tasks implemented with TDD, 21 new tests passing
description: Implement core TDT greedy decode algorithm in pure Go. Orchestrates Decoder and JointDecision models with LSTM state management, duration bins, and max-symbols-per-step guard.
title: TDT greedy decode loop

description: AES-256-GCM is currently called with nil AAD on both Go and ESP32 sides. The packetNum field in DataPacket is not cryptographically bound to the ciphertext, meaning it could be altered without detection. Pass packetNum as Additional Authenticated Data to aesGCM.Seal/Open (Go) and psa_aead_decrypt (ESP32) so tampering with packet numbers is detected.
title: Bind packetNum into AES-GCM AAD for authenticated packet numbering

close_reason: All changes applied to README.md

title: Fix Go version requirement in README (says 1.21+, needs 1.25+)

description: After 'task ble-pair' succeeds, the developer must manually edit config.yaml to set inject.method: ble. Either automate this in the pairing flow or make the manual step more prominent in the docs.
title: Auto-set inject.method to ble after successful pairing

description: The ECDH key exchange during pairing has no authentication — an active attacker present during pairing could intercept both public keys. Add a numeric comparison step (display 6-digit code on both Mac and ESP32 LED) so the user can verify the pairing is not being intercepted. This is the highest-value security hardening item.
title: Add numeric comparison during BLE pairing to prevent MITM

close_reason: Implemented BLE client core send logic with tests. All 7 client tests + 22 existing BLE tests pass.
description: Implement the BLE client that encrypts text, chunks it, and writes to the TX characteristic. Files: internal/ble/client.go, internal/ble/client_test.go. Depends on Tasks 1-3 (protocol, chunking, crypto) and Task 5 (adapter interfaces).
title: Task 6: BLE Client — Core Send Logic

close_reason: All 6 scaffold files created and committed
description: Create the ESP-IDF project structure at firmware/esp32/. This is the foundation all other firmware tasks build on. Files: top-level CMakeLists.txt, main/CMakeLists.txt, main/main.c (NVS init), sdkconfig.defaults (USB-OTG, NimBLE, TinyUSB, mbedtls), partitions.csv, main/config.h (BLE UUIDs, timing, NVS keys, crypto sizes).
title: ESP32-S3 project scaffold (CMake, NVS, config)

close_reason: Fixed by running listener.Start() on the main OS thread (with runtime.LockOSThread()) and moving the event processing select loop to a goroutine. This ensures CGEventTap runs on the main thread, making event_loop == CFRunLoopGetMain() in hook_run(), which skips the dispatch_sync_f(main_queue,...) deadlock path.
description: On macOS, gohook's hook_run() calls dispatch_sync_f(main_queue, ...) on every keypress to perform Unicode character lookup. Since listener.Start() is called from a goroutine (not the main OS thread), dispatch_sync_f deadlocks because Go's main goroutine never pumps the GCD main queue. This causes the CGEventTap to timeout and be disabled, leaving the app unresponsive to key events and preventing clean exit.\n\nFix: Run listener.Start() on the main OS thread (with runtime.LockOSThread()) by moving the event processing loop to a separate goroutine. This makes event_loop == CFRunLoopGetMain(), which skips the dispatch_sync_f path in process_key_pressed and calls keycode_to_lookup directly instead.
title: Fix: gohook CGEventTap deadlocks main queue on every keypress

description:
When holding the record hotkey (ctrl+shift+r), keystrokes pass through to the terminal and get echoed. Root cause: gohook's async architecture means the C-level CGEventTap callback returns events to the OS before Go code can suppress them. The event.reserved consumption mechanism exists in C but is never triggered by dispatch_proc.

Options (simplest to most involved):
1. Change default hotkey to something that doesn't produce terminal output (F5, F13-F24, modifier-only combo)
2. Fork gohook and add synchronous key filtering in C dispatch_proc (return NULL for matching combos)
3. Replace gohook with a custom minimal CGEventTap wrapper that does synchronous filtering in C
4. Post-hoc cleanup: send backspace keystrokes after recording stops (fragile)

Relevant code: internal/hotkey/hotkey.go, gohook v0.42.3 hook_c.h (CGEventTapCreate with kCGEventTapOptionDefault)
title: Hotkey keypresses echo to terminal during recording
